title: calluna vulgaris
intro: Calluna vulgaris, also known as common heather, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to Europe, particularly in acidic, well-drained soils and moorlands. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of common heather include reducing coughs and colds, as well as providing relief from menstrual cramps and menopausal symptoms. Common heather is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
